title: Artifact "SBOMs" tab is a Versions tab — paginate, label, and count by version
description:
## Problem

The "SBOMs" tab on the artifact detail page mislabels and miscounts what it shows, and its pagination is broken. Surfaced by the Traefik artifact, where the footer says "Showing 1-25 of 39" but only ~4 rows are visible.

These are two faces of the same problem: the tab is conceptually a Versions tab, but it is wired to a SBOM-paginated API and labelled with the raw SBOM count.

## Evidence

- API GET /api/v1/artifacts/{id}/sboms (internal/api/search.go:282) returns paginated raw SBOM rows. Query ListSBOMsByArtifact in db/queries/artifact.sql:50-69 uses COUNT(*) OVER() over raw SBOMs and ORDER BY s.created_at DESC LIMIT/OFFSET.
- Frontend SBOMsTab (web/src/pages/ArtifactDetail/SBOMsTab.tsx:21-24) calls groupSBOMsByVersionAndArch (web/src/utils/groupSBOMs.ts:8-27), keeping only the newest SBOM per (subject_version, architecture). For Traefik, 39 raw SBOMs collapse to 4 unique versions.
- Pagination (SBOMsTab.tsx:262-265) reflects the raw count (39), not the displayed row count.
- Paging "next" advances by raw-SBOM offset; older revisions of the same 4 versions can be returned, again collapsing — pagination does not actually reach distinct content.
- Tab button (ArtifactDetail/index.tsx:227-234) reads "SBOMs ({a().sbomCount})" — SBOM-flavored label and count for a version-flavored table.

## Root cause

Vocabulary drift. The page was built around versions but kept SBOM nomenclature and a SBOM-paginated API.

## Fix direction

Introduce GET /api/v1/artifacts/{id}/versions that: paginates by subject_version, rolls up architectures and newest revision per version server-side, returns a version count. Frontend: rename tab to Versions, switch to new endpoint, drop client-side grouping.

## Affected files

- internal/api/search.go
- internal/service/search_sbom.go
- db/queries/artifact.sql
- web/src/pages/ArtifactDetail/index.tsx
- web/src/pages/ArtifactDetail/SBOMsTab.tsx
- web/src/utils/groupSBOMs.ts
- web/src/api/queries/artifacts.ts
notes:
Files: db/queries/artifact.sql, internal/repository/artifact.sql.go (generated), internal/repository/repository.go, internal/service/search.go, internal/service/search_artifact.go, internal/api/types.go, internal/api/search.go, internal/api/router.go, web/src/api/client.ts, web/src/api/queries/artifacts.ts, web/src/api/queries/index.ts, web/src/pages/ArtifactDetail/VersionsTab.tsx (new), web/src/pages/ArtifactDetail/index.tsx
Approach: Added GET /api/v1/artifacts/{id}/versions with two-CTE SQL query (sboms_meta + DISTINCT ON for newest-per-version + array_agg for architectures). CountArtifactVersions feeds versionCount into ArtifactDetail. Frontend: replaced SBOMsTab with VersionsTab (server-side grouping, no client-side groupSBOMsByVersionAndArch), tab renamed to Versions (N). Deleted SBOMsTab.tsx and groupSBOMs.ts.
close_reason: Closed

title: 1.0 — Solid Core
description: Refactor and stabilize the OCIDex core. No new feature surfaces. The yardstick: a 1.2 CLI or 1.3 operator should consume the API + Go packages without further refactors.\n\nSections: A (RBAC hardening), B (interface extraction), C (test coverage lift), D (frontend decomposition), E (ephemeral-job foundations), F (kustomize dev setup), G (API surface review), H (documentation).

title: diff-tree endpoint: combine diff+deps in one backend call
description: The build history tree view currently makes two API calls per build pair: the diff endpoint and the full dep graph endpoint. The dep graph response includes 10,158 nodes of which 9,560 (94%) are file-type and immediately discarded. Add GET /api/v1/sboms/diff-tree?from=&to= that returns the diff changes plus the non-file dep graph of the 'to' SBOM in one call.
notes:
Files: db/queries/search.sql, internal/service/search.go, internal/service/changelog.go, internal/api/types.go, internal/api/search.go, internal/api/router.go, internal/api/search_test.go, web/src/api/queries/diff.ts, web/src/api/queries/index.ts, web/src/api/client.ts, web/src/components/DiffTreeView.tsx, web/src/pages/ArtifactVersionHistory.tsx
Approach: Added ListSBOMPackages SQL query (type != file filter at DB level). New DiffSBOMsWithTree service method runs diff and dep graph fetch in one go, using non-file packages for both. New GET /api/v1/sboms/diff-tree endpoint returns DiffTree (diff+graph combined). Frontend replaces useDiff+useSBOMDependencies with useDiffTree; one HTTP call, backend sends only 598 nodes instead of 10158.
close_reason: Closed

title: Jobs tab: error detail collapses on every data refresh
description:
In Admin > Jobs, clicking 'View error' on a failed job opens a <details> element showing the error text. However, useListScanJobs has refetchInterval: 2500, which triggers a full table re-render every 2.5 seconds. Because <details> is an uncontrolled element in Solid, each re-render resets it to its default closed state, collapsing the expanded error immediately.

**Root cause:**
The <details> element in the Last Error column (Admin.tsx:944–950) is uncontrolled HTML. Solid's reconciliation replaces the DOM node on each query result update, losing the open attribute.

**Fix:**
Replace the uncontrolled <details> with a controlled expand/collapse using createSignal per job row. Track a Set or Map of expanded job IDs in the JobsTab component, and use a Show + button pattern instead of <details>/<summary>.

Example approach:
- In JobsTab, add: const [expandedErrors, setExpandedErrors] = createSignal(new Set<string>())
- In the row: replace <details> with a button that toggles the job id in the set, and a Show component that renders the error text when the id is present
- Use untrack or a stable keyed accessor so the signal survives the query update

**Affected file:**
- web/src/pages/Admin.tsx — JobsTab, the 'Last Error' column cell (lines ~943–951)
close_reason: replaced uncontrolled <details> with Set<string> signal in JobsTab scope; expanded state survives query refetches

title: Architecture switcher shows duplicates for Windows multi-arch SBOMs
description:
## Problem

When clicking into a Windows multi-arch artifact version (/sboms/{id}), the architecture switcher shows duplicate buttons (e.g., "amd64", "amd64", "amd64", "arm64"). Same problem appears in the Architectures column of the artifact detail Versions table.

## Evidence

- Arch switcher (web/src/pages/SBOMDetail/index.tsx:276-290) renders one button per sibling SBOM with the same subjectVersion. No dedup on architecture.
- Scanner ociExpandIndex (internal/scanner/catalog.go:360-380) parses each manifest in an OCI image index but stores only arch. Windows multi-arch indexes contain multiple manifests sharing architecture: amd64 but differing in os.version.
- ociGetImageMetadata (internal/scanner/catalog.go:391-470) reads only config.Architecture — same problem.
- Result: multiple SBOMs per Windows variant; identical architecture enrichment; UI renders each as a separate "amd64" button.

## Root cause

Scanner and enrichment discard os and os.version. No qualifier capable of distinguishing Windows variants.

## Fix direction (two layers)

1. Scanner/enrichment: capture os and os.version alongside architecture. Option (a): extend enrichment record with explicit os/os.version fields, update SBOM list query. Option (b): composite platform string (e.g., windows/amd64/10.0.17763) when os == windows.
2. Frontend defense: dedupe arch siblings by platform identifier in SBOMDetail/index.tsx:276-290 and SBOMsTab.tsx:178-194.

## Affected files

- internal/scanner/catalog.go
- internal/enrichment/ (oci-metadata enricher)
- db/queries/artifact.sql (if option 1a)
- web/src/pages/SBOMDetail/index.tsx
- web/src/pages/ArtifactDetail/SBOMsTab.tsx
notes: Files: internal/enrichment/oci/oci.go, web/src/pages/SBOMDetail/index.tsx\nApproach: Two-layer fix. (1) Enricher: added platformArchitecture() helper that builds composite 'windows/amd64/10.0.17763' string when OS=windows and os.version is present, using cfg.OSVersion from go-containerregistry v1.ConfigFile. Stored in enrichment JSONB architecture field; SQL queries unchanged. (2) Frontend: archSiblings() now deduplicates by architecture using a Set — handles existing SBOMs re-enriched before this fix and any edge cases.
close_reason: Closed

title: Structured lifecycle logs for --once mode workers
description: Add structured slog lifecycle log lines to scanner-worker and enrichment-worker --once mode: log start, image/sbom being processed, completion status, and duration. Makes ephemeral job logs parseable by log aggregators.\n\nAffected: cmd/scanner-worker/main.go, cmd/enrichment-worker/main.go.
notes: Files: cmd/scanner-worker/main.go, cmd/enrichment-worker/main.go\nApproach: Added structured slog lifecycle lines to runOnce and runEnrichOnce. Scanner logs: scan started (image/repo/digest/tag), scan complete (duration_ms), ingest complete (total_duration_ms). Enrichment logs: enrichment started (sbom_id), enriching SBOM (sbom_id, artifact_name), enrichment complete (sbom_id, duration_ms). All log lines use nolint gosec G706 since env vars are trusted.
close_reason: Closed

title: Integration test: Nats-Msg-Id dedup on redelivery
description: Integration test verifying that a redelivered message (simulated by a consumer crash mid-processing) is correctly deduplicated by Nats-Msg-Id and not processed twice. Use testcontainers + NATS.\n\nAffected: tests/ integration test suite.

title: Audit log login/logout/session events
description: Add audit log entries for login, logout, and session events. There is an existing audit log implementation (internal/audit/) but login/logout events are not captured — gap identified after the initial audit logging work.\n\nAffected: internal/audit/, auth handlers in internal/api/.
notes:
Files: internal/event/event.go, internal/service/auth.go, internal/service/auth_test.go, internal/audit/extension.go, internal/audit/extension_test.go, cmd/ocidex/main.go, tests/auth_integration_test.go
Approach: Added UserLoggedIn/UserLoggedOut event types and data structs to the event package. Injected event.Publisher into authService (matches pattern in sbomService). Publish UserLoggedIn from CreateSession after successful session creation; publish UserLoggedOut from DeleteSession by looking up user via ValidateSession before deletion (non-fatal if lookup fails). Subscribed both new event types in the audit extension. Updated all call sites and added noopPublisher in auth_test.go.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add per-message timeout to ProcessOne in NATS extensions
description: Add a per-message processing timeout to the ProcessOne method in internal/extension/ NATS handlers. Prevents a single slow message from blocking the consumer indefinitely.\n\nAffected: internal/extension/nats.go or similar NATS extension consumer.
notes: Files: internal/scanner/nats_extension.go, internal/enrichment/nats_extension.go, cmd/scanner-worker/main.go, cmd/enrichment-worker/main.go, tests/nats_integration_test.go, tests/scan_to_enrich_test.go\nApproach: Added msgTimeout time.Duration field to both NATSExtension structs. NewNATSExtension now takes msgTimeout as a parameter. The processing goroutines wrap ProcessOne with context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), e.msgTimeout) so hung goroutines release the semaphore slot before JetStream redelivers. Scanner uses 9m (AckWait 10m - 1m), enrichment uses 4m (AckWait 5m - 1m). Test call sites updated with matching timeouts.
close_reason: Closed

title: 1.0.B.14 — Catalog walks via message queue
description: Route catalog walk results through the NATS message queue instead of direct in-process dispatch. This decouples the poller from the scanner and enables distributed catalog walking.\n\nAffected: internal/scanner/catalog.go, internal/nats/, cmd/scanner-worker.

title: 1.0.D.6 — Surface 'scan untagged images' toggle on registry config
description: Add a toggle to the registry admin UI and API to enable scanning untagged images. Many OCI artifacts use annotations/labels that are only on untagged manifests; this expands what ocidex can index.\n\nAffected files: registry admin UI (web/src/pages/Admin.tsx), registry API handler, registry service, db schema (scan_untagged column).

title: 1.0.B.12 — Dead-letter stream + scan_job_failures
description: Add a NATS dead-letter stream for failed scan jobs. When a scan job exceeds max delivery attempts, move it to a DLQ stream and record in scan_job_failures table for operator review.\n\nAffected: internal/nats/, internal/scanner/, new scan_job_failures migration.

title: scan_jobs table + lifecycle service
description: Add scan_jobs table and lifecycle service to track scan job state (pending → running → done/failed). Enables scan progress UI in admin panel.
close_reason: Done: scan_jobs table and lifecycle service added; scan progress UI in admin (PR #48)

title: Poller leader election via pg advisory lock
description: Add leader election for the registry poller using PostgreSQL advisory locks to prevent multiple instances from polling simultaneously in distributed mode.
close_reason: Done: poller leader election via pg advisory lock (PR #50)

title: Synchronous NATS publish on API webhook path
description: Switch the API webhook scan trigger to synchronous NATS publish (wait for ack) instead of fire-and-forget, so 202 means the job was queued.
close_reason: Done: switched API webhook to synchronous NATS publish (PR #46)

title: Nats-Msg-Id dedup on submit + relay
description: Add Nats-Msg-Id header on publish (submit) and relay paths to enable JetStream deduplication. Affects internal/nats/ and scanner submission path.
close_reason: Done: Nats-Msg-Id dedup on submit + relay paths (PR #47)

title: Move Ack to after processing in NATS extensions
description: Fix premature Ack in internal/extension/nats.go: move Ack to after processing completes to restore at-least-once delivery guarantees.
close_reason: Done: moved Ack to after processing in NATS extension handler (PR #51)

title: Service unit tests for auth.go and registry.go

close_reason: Done: service unit tests for auth.go, registry.go, and helpers (PR #45)

close_reason: Done: --once mode added to cmd/scanner-worker; reads SCAN_IMAGE+SCAN_REGISTRY_ID env, exits 0/1 (PR #49)

(2) Added a Changelog button column to VersionsTab linking to /artifacts/:id/versions/:version (ArtifactVersionHistory page). (3) Sorted licenses by componentCount descending in LicensesTab.

Not implemented: 'license display at SBOM level' — requires a new backend endpoint /api/v1/sboms/{id}/license-summary which does not exist yet. Should be a separate issue.

Types, constants, and helpers (RegType, TYPE_CAPS, emptyForm, toPatternArray) were co-located with RegistriesTab. Build + tsc + ESLint all pass.

title: normalizeRegistryHost fails for full URL inputs (strips scheme)
description:
If a registry is stored with a full URL (e.g. https://quay.io), the scanner's normalizeRegistryHost passes it through unchanged, causing API calls to fail. Fix strips the scheme prefix so callers that store full URLs still work.

Affected: internal/scanner/catalog.go

Discovered while testing with public registry scripts.
close_reason: Fixed in normalizeRegistryHost: strip scheme prefix before the switch; committed in 5af64bb

title: Spike: tree view animation and expand-all without browser crash
description:
## Why this exists

Two attempts to add smooth animation and expand-all to the dependency tree view (ocidex-0fp) both caused browser crashes. This spike should produce a concrete, validated approach before any implementation.

## What was tried

### Attempt 1 — always-mount all descendants
Pre-render the entire tree in the DOM (collapsed via CSS) so expand/collapse can animate without mount/unmount latency. Used CSS grid-template-rows: 0fr/1fr transition.

**Result:** Crashed immediately. A 500-package SBOM creates 500+ TreeNodeRow components each with createSignal + createMemo + createEffect + a recursive Set copy (nextVisited). The browser tab ran out of memory before painting anything.

### Attempt 2 — lazy-mount + cascade expand-all via createEffect(on(...))
Kept <Show when={expanded()}> for lazy mount. Added a SolidJS context carrying collapse/expand counter signals. Each TreeNodeRow ran createEffect(on(ctx.expand, ...)) without defer so newly-mounted children would also expand immediately — cascading depth-first until all nodes were expanded.

**Result:** Expand All still crashed. Rapidly mounting hundreds of components in a synchronous reactive cascade overwhelms the browser even with lazy mount. Collapse All worked fine (just sets expanded=false on mounted nodes).

## Root causes identified

1. **Reactive component count scales with O(visible nodes × depth)** — each TreeNodeRow allocates several reactive primitives; for large SBOMs even the lazy-mount approach can mount too many in one synchronous batch.

2. **nextVisited memo is O(depth) per node** — creates a new Set at every depth level, copying all ancestors. For a flat tree of 500 nodes each at depth 1 this is cheap, but real package DAGs can have shared nodes reached via long paths.

3. **Expand-all cascade is unbounded** — triggering a reactive signal that causes N mounts, each immediately running an effect that causes more mounts, is a synchronous avalanche.

## What to research

- **Centralized expansion state**: lift the Set<nodeRef> of expanded nodes to the tree root instead of per-node signals. Expand All becomes a single Set.add of all refs with no reactivity cascade. TreeNodeRow reads from this set reactively.

- **Virtualization**: render only the visible rows (windowed/virtual list). AG Grid, TanStack Virtual, or a simpler custom approach. Each row is a fixed-height div; only rows in the viewport are mounted.

- **Expand-all without cascade**: if expansion state is centralized, expand-all can be a single batch: compute all reachable non-cyclic refs, fill the Set, re-render. Children are still lazy but their "should expand" state is already in the Set when they mount.

- **Animation without always-mount**: CSS @starting-style (Chrome 117+, Firefox 129+) allows entry transitions even on first mount. Would let us use max-height or opacity transitions on lazy-mounted children without always-mounting.

- **Flatten then render**: instead of recursive component tree, flatten the visible rows into an array at the tree root (depth-first, filtered by expanded set), render with a single For loop over flat rows. This gives O(1) component overhead per node, enables windowing, and makes expand-all a pure data operation.

## Acceptance criteria

Chosen approach is validated in a local prototype (not necessarily the full implementation). Document: the approach, the library/pattern used (if any), and any trade-offs around animation vs. expand-all vs. virtualization.

title: Jobs tab: default filter to running/queued, add state and registry filter controls
description:
Admin > Jobs currently shows ALL jobs with no filtering UI, paginated but unfiltered. In normal operation the most interesting view is active jobs (running + queued). Completed and failed jobs are only relevant for debugging. Also, the ScanJobResponse has registry_id and repository fields that aren't used for filtering today.

**What needs to change:**

1. Default state filter: initialise the state filter signal to show running+queued jobs (pass state='running' by default to useListScanJobs). Note: the API only accepts a single state value — to show both running and queued, either fetch both in parallel and merge client-side, or default to 'running' only and let users select 'queued' separately. Prefer the two-request approach for accuracy; merge and sort by created_at desc.

2. Filter bar (above the table): add controls for:
   - State: segmented toggle or a <select> with options: All | Queued | Running | Succeeded | Failed (default: Running)
   - Search/filter by repository string (text input, client-side filter on job.repository and job.tag)
   - Registry: a <select> populated from useListRegistries(), filtering client-side by registry_id (since the API does not support this param today). Show 'All registries' as default.

3. Show job counts per state somewhere (can borrow from the scan_jobs stats already shown in the Status tab).

**Affected files:**
- web/src/pages/Admin.tsx — JobsTab function and useListScanJobs call
- web/src/api/queries/jobs.ts — may need to support parallel queries or rethink the accessor API

title: Registries: hide webhook-specific actions for poll-only registries
description:
In Admin > Registries, the table always shows 'Copy URL' and 'Regen Secret' buttons for every registry, even for types that do not support webhooks (docker and ghcr have webhook: false in TYPE_CAPS). This is confusing — those registries are poll-only and have no webhook URL or secret to manage.

**What needs to change:**
Conditionally render the webhook action buttons based on whether the registry actually uses webhooks. A registry uses webhooks when scan_mode is 'webhook' or 'both', AND when its type supports it (TYPE_CAPS[type].webhook === true).

The column/cell should:
- Still always render the Webhook URL column (to avoid layout shifts), but show a '-' or empty cell for poll-only registries
- Hide both 'Copy URL' and 'Regen Secret' buttons when the registry's effective scan_mode is 'poll' or the registry type has webhook: false

Condition to use (on each row):
  const hasWebhook = (reg) => reg.scan_mode !== 'poll' && TYPE_CAPS[reg.type as RegType]?.webhook === true

Wrap the two webhook buttons in a Show component using this predicate.

**Affected file:**
- web/src/pages/Admin.tsx — RegistriesTab table row, the 'Webhook URL' cell (lines ~824–843)

title: Compare page: show architecture and build time in SBOM selection dropdowns
description:
The SBOM picker dropdowns on /diff currently use sbomLabel() which only shows 'version · date' or 'date · N components'. When an artifact has multiple SBOMs for the same version (e.g. multi-arch builds), the options are nearly indistinguishable. Architecture and build time are already present on SBOMSummary (fields: architecture and buildDate).

**What needs to change:**
Update the option labels in both the From and To SBOM selects in Diff.tsx to include architecture and build time when present.

Suggested label format:
- With version + arch + buildDate: '24.04 · amd64 · built Jan 15, 2025, 3:42 PM'
- With version + arch only: '24.04 · amd64 · ingested Jan 15, 2025'
- With version only (no arch): '24.04 · Jan 15, 2025'
- Fallback (no version): 'Jan 15, 2025, 3:42 PM · 342 components'

Implement a new sbomPickerLabel(sbom: SBOMSummary) helper in utils/format.ts that produces this richer label. Use it only in Diff.tsx option elements (not sbomLabel which is used in other contexts).

**Affected files:**
- web/src/utils/format.ts — add sbomPickerLabel()
- web/src/pages/Diff.tsx — use sbomPickerLabel() for option text

title: Package view: filter plan/file-type components by default, with show toggle
description:
The flat PackagesTab already hard-filters components where type === 'file', but the DependencyTreeView does not — it renders every node from the raw dependency graph including build-plan entries (syft-detected .apkbuild, .spec, Nix .drv, and other file-type nodes). These inflate the tree and obscure the real package list.

**What needs to change:**
Apply a consistent plan-file filter across ALL views that display packages:
1. DependencyTreeView: filter out nodes from the dependency graph where the corresponding ComponentSummary has type === 'file' OR a purl type of 'file', before building the tree adjacency list.
2. PackagesTab (flat): already filters type === 'file'; no change needed for the filter logic itself, but expose the toggle to the user.
3. DiffEntry (compare/build-history): the 'Packages only' checkbox already filters to items with a purl, but plan files (type 'file') can still appear. Add a secondary filter that hides purl type === 'file' items when the toggle is on.

**UI:**
Add a 'Show plan files' toggle (default off) to:
- The filter bar in the package view (PackagesTab / tree view toolbar)
- The filter bar in DiffEntry (alongside the existing 'Packages only' checkbox)

**Affected files:**
- web/src/pages/SBOMDetail/PackagesTab.tsx
- web/src/components/DiffEntry.tsx
- web/src/pages/ArtifactVersionHistory.tsx (passes props to DiffEntry)
- web/src/pages/Diff.tsx (passes props to DiffEntry)
notes:
Files: web/src/components/DiffTreeView.tsx (new), web/src/pages/ArtifactVersionHistory.tsx
Approach: Created DiffTreeView component that fetches the 'to' SBOM's dep graph and shows diff changes as an annotated dependency tree. Nodes with changes show a badge; unchanged ancestors of changed nodes show dimmed with child-count badge; tree auto-expands to first changed nodes. Removed packages not in the new graph appear as flat orphan rows. ArtifactVersionHistory now has a Tree/List toggle (default: Tree) that fetches the dep graph per BuildDiffEntry pair.
close_reason: Closed

title: Package view: tree-first layout with rich node detail
description:
The SBOM detail page currently has two separate tabs — a flat table (Packages) and a basic tree (Dependency Tree). The tree shows only name + dep-count badge; the flat table has version, type badge, and PURL. Users lose all the useful column detail when switching to tree view.

**What needs to change:**
Collapse the two tabs into a single view. The primary layout should be the dependency tree (collapsed by default), but each node must carry the same details currently shown in the flat table: version, ecosystem/type badge, and PURL. A toggle in the toolbar should let users switch to the classic flat list.

**Specific design:**
- Tree mode (default): roots shown collapsed. Each row shows name (linked), version (mono), type badge, dep-count badge. Expanding a node reveals its direct deps as indented rows with the same detail columns.
- Flat mode: existing PackagesTab table, unchanged.
- Toggle: a small segmented control or icon button (Tree | List) in the filter bar.
- The DependencyTreeView component currently renders in a fixed 600px max-height div with no columns — replace that row-by-row approach with a proper table layout where the indent/expand toggle occupies the Name column.

**Affected files:**
- web/src/pages/SBOMDetail/PackagesTab.tsx — DependencyTreeView, PackagesTab, TreeNodeRow
- web/src/pages/SBOMDetail/index.tsx — remove separate Dependencies tab, pass combined view

**Out of scope:** diff/compare pages (covered by a separate issue)
notes:
Files: web/src/pages/SBOMDetail/PackagesTab.tsx, web/src/pages/SBOMDetail/index.tsx
Approach: Collapsed Packages+Dependencies tabs into a single view. PackagesTab now accepts optional depsGraph prop; when deps data with edges is available, Tree/List toggle appears in the filter bar and tree is shown by default (roots collapsed). DependencyTreeView rebuilt as a proper <table> with Name/Version/Type/Deps columns. TreeNodeRow renders <tr> elements with depth-based padding in the Name cell. Deps always eagerly loaded; tab signal and tab-bar removed from index.tsx.
close_reason: Collapsed Packages+Dependencies tabs into unified tree-first view with Tree/List toggle

title: 1.4 — Terraform provider (cmd/terraform-provider-ocidex)
description: Plugin Framework provider with resources mirroring the CRDs: ocidex_registry, ocidex_api_key. Built on the same Go OpenAPI client from 1.2.

title: 1.3 — K8s Operator + CRDs
description: Built with controller-runtime / kubebuilder layout inside the monorepo. CRDs: Registry (declarative registry registration), ScanRequest (one-shot scan via ephemeral Job using --once contract), APIKey (optional, declarative API keys written to Secret).

title: 1.2 — CLI tool (cmd/ocidex-cli)
description: Cobra-based CLI consuming the generated OpenAPI client. Subcommands: ocidex scan <image>, ocidex ingest <sbom.json>, ocidex search components, ocidex registry add/list, ocidex artifact history. Auth via OCIDEX_API_KEY env or ocidex login.

title: 1.1 — Production K8s deployment
description: Helm chart wrapping k8s/base/, Ingress with TLS, secrets via External Secrets Operator or sealed-secrets, RBAC + ServiceAccounts + NetworkPolicy, Prometheus metrics endpoint, structured-log shipping to Loki. Built on the kustomize base from 1.0.F.

title: 1.0.H.5 — README pass: align with current state
description: README pass: align with current state (workers, NATS modes, kustomize dev setup, OCIDEX_MODE config).

title: 1.0.H.4 — ADR 0021: ephemeral job contract
description: Add docs/adr/0021-ephemeral-job-contract.md documenting the --once mode env/exit-code contract used by scanner-worker and enrichment-worker.

title: 1.0.H.3 — ADR 0020: pluggable enricher interface
description: Add docs/adr/0020-enricher-interface.md documenting the pluggable enrichment registry (Name/Supports/Enrich interface, registration at startup).

title: 1.0.H.1 — Update docs/DEVELOPMENT.md to reflect huma handler pattern
description: Update docs/DEVELOPMENT.md to reflect the huma handler pattern (current examples are stale per prior exploration).

title: 1.0.H.2 — ADR 0019: RBAC / visibility model
description: Add docs/adr/0019-rbac-visibility.md documenting the auth/ownership model (registry owner, public/private visibility, admin vs member roles).

title: 1.0.G.2 — API versioning policy (docs/API_VERSIONING.md)
description: Confirm /api/v1/ prefix is honored; document in docs/API_VERSIONING.md how breaking changes will be handled (v2 namespace) and what counts as a breaking change.

title: 1.0.F.5 — Externalize hardcoded ocidex:8080 in zot config
description: Externalize the hardcoded ocidex:8080 in config/zot/config.json via env-var substitution so the same config works in compose and kustomize.

title: 1.0.F.3 — Document dev K8s loop in K8S_DEV.md
description: Document a complete dev loop in docs/K8S_DEV.md: kind create cluster → kustomize build k8s/overlays/dev | kubectl apply -f - → port-forward → seed → frontend dev server points at port-forwarded API.

title: 1.0.F.4 — Add make k8s-dev-up / k8s-dev-down / k8s-dev-logs
description: Add make targets: make k8s-dev-up, make k8s-dev-down, make k8s-dev-logs for the kustomize dev workflow.

title: 1.0.F.2 — k8s/base/secrets.yaml template
description: Add k8s/base/secrets.yaml template + .gitignore entry; document kustomize edit add secret workflow.

title: 1.0.E.3 — Document ephemeral job contract
description: Document the --once contract (env vars, exit codes) in docs/EPHEMERAL_JOBS.md. Operator (1.3) and CLI (1.2) consume this contract.
